page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-to-core ratio, backed by 128 GB of RAM providing 2.0 GB per core. The CPU includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX-512_VNNI, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance operates on a KVM hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps, but does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the instance scoring in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU evaluations. It demonstrates high efficiency in parallelized workloads, showing strong results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read operations are highly efficient, whereas uncached large-block read and write operations fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and database operations also perform strongly, as evidenced by high PassMark database and encryption scores.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c7t.16xlarge is its compute-heavy, memory-lean configuration. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and no local storage, it is not designed for memory-intensive databases or large-scale local data caching. However, its resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suited for parallel processing tasks, static web serving, software build pipelines, and moderate-sized LLM inference tasks, such as text generation with 7B parameter models, where it achieves strong performance relative to its hardware class.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, and features a 32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations. While single-core and uncached memory bandwidth metrics are average, its support for AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions enhances parallel processing efficiency. This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including static web serving, software build pipelines, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 32 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput parallel workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 32 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core compute-optimized platform delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high-speed networking for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores at 2.7 GHz
Features 128 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 32 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations
Supports AVX-512, AVX-512_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Best suited for compute-bound parallel workloads, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference
